rules: [P1] Do not create medication orders unless the relevant trigger condition (e.g., active opioid order for naloxone) is present.
[P1] Always verify current active orders before creating a new one.
[P1] Retrieve and use the most recent relevant lab values or clinical dates (e.g., immunization) before making protocol-driven decisions.

[P1] Search thoroughly for prior relevant procedures, orders, medications, device actions, observations, or lab values using all protocol-specified codes, synonyms, sources, and within specified time windows before making ordering or clinical decisions.
[P1] Check elapsed time since last relevant intervention or device placement per protocol before ordering.
[P1] Take no action if all values are within protocol-defined normal ranges and no intervention is indicated.
[P1] Do not create removal orders if no active device or procedure exists.

[P1] Only create, order, or discontinue new procedures, interventions, devices, or medications if no prior, active, or protocol-equivalent orders are found, or if existing ones are outdated per protocol.
[P1] Recommend ordering missing labs before making protocol decisions.
[P1] Explicitly handle missing data scenarios: report missing values, state if protocol dictates alternative workflow, and recommend remedial or follow-up actions.
[P1] Take no action if protocol requirements are already met.
[P1] After any order change, confirm there is exactly one active order and no duplicates.
[P1] Discontinue duplicates, keeping only the most recent order.
